title: Attention in Translation
concept: During translation, attention shifts left-to-right along the sentence, showing the decoder which source word to translate next.

options.text: To focus on the right words at the right time.
To check the spelling of the translation.

exercises.explain: Attention helps the AI focus on the most relevant parts of the input text when generating each translated word.

cards.title: Step-by-Step Translation
cards.body: How does an AI translator know which word to translate next in a long sentence? It doesn't just guess; it uses a virtual spotlight that scans the sentence from left to right!

cards.title: What is Attention?
cards.body: In translation, attention is a mechanism that helps the computer focus on specific words in the original sentence. As it generates the translation, this focus shifts step-by-step from left to right, matching the natural flow of the sentence.

cards.title: The Reading Finger
cards.body: Think of attention like a reading guide's finger pointing to words in a Spanish book while writing the English translation. As you write each English word, your finger slides along the Spanish sentence from left to right, pointing to the exact word you need to translate next.

cards.title: Translating French to English
cards.body: When translating 'Je suis fatigué' to 'I am tired', the attention spotlight first focuses on 'Je' to write 'I', then shifts right to 'suis' to write 'am', and finally moves to 'fatigué' to write 'tired'.

cards.title: The 'All at Once' Myth
cards.body: People often think AI translation models look at the entire sentence all at once for every single word they output. In reality, the AI uses attention to shift its focus from left to right, focusing on one key part of the source sentence at a time.

cards.title: The Shift in Action
cards.body: See how the focus of the translation engine moves as it works through a sentence.

left.text: Attention focuses on the first word on the left.

right.text: Attention has shifted all the way to the right.

exercises.prompt: What is the main job of attention during translation?

options.text: It deletes words that are too difficult to translate.
It shows the decoder which source word to translate next.
It translates the entire sentence into numbers instantly.
It rearranges the original sentence into alphabetical order.

exercises.explain: Attention helps the decoder by highlighting which word in the source sentence it should focus on next.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The attention spotlight stays locked on the very first word of the sentence during the entire translation.
exercises.explain: Attention shifts dynamically from left to right as the translation is generated, rather than staying in one fixed spot.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence to describe how attention moves during translation.
exercises.sentence: During translation, the attention spotlight moves from left to _____.
exercises.explain: As translation happens, attention moves step-by-step from left to right along the source sentence.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is using attention?

options.text: An AI translator that shifts its focus along a sentence to translate it naturally.
A program that counts the number of letters in a sentence.

exercises.explain: An AI translator uses attention to focus on specific words one by one, while a simple dictionary just replaces words without looking at the context or flow.

exercises.prompt: How does attention behave during the translation process?

options.text: It focuses on all source words equally at all times.
It moves left-to-right along the source sentence, highlighting the next word to translate.
It randomly jumps to different parts of the sentence without any order.
It only focuses on the very last word of the source sentence.

exercises.explain: As the decoder builds the translated sentence step-by-step, the attention mechanism acts like a moving spotlight, shifting from left to right across the source sentence to highlight the next word to translate.
